perturbation.original_text: FASC agrees to provide to the Adviser the services indicated in Exhibit A to this Agreement (the "Services").
perturbation.changed_text: FASC agrees to provide to the Adviser certain services as reasonably requested by the Adviser.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly defines that the 'Services' are indicated in Exhibit A, which gives a specific and limited scope of services. The modified text replaces this with a vague commitment to provide services 'as reasonably requested,' omitting the reference to Exhibit A. This creates uncertainty and contradicts the later sections that reference Exhibit A for defining the scope and fees for Services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For each Category of Services referenced in Exhibit A, Adviser shall pay FASC a Services Fee, payable monthly in arrears, determined according to the following formula: Services Fee = Cost of Services x Adviser's Assets under Management Total Assets Under Management x (1 + Applicable Margin) Where: "Cost of Services" is FASC's total Operating Costs incurred in providing the applicable Category of Services during the month to all investment advisers for which FASC provides that Category of Services. "Adviser's Assets under Management" is the total average assets under management for the month for all Accounts or portions thereof for which the Adviser acts as investment adviser or subadvisor and which utilize the Category of Services. "Total Assets under Management" is the total average assets under management for the month for all Accounts or portions thereof for which all investment advisers (including the Adviser) to which FASC provides that Category of Services act as investment adviser or subadviser and which utilize the Category of Services. "Applicable Margin" is 0.10. "Operating Costs" means all operating expenses and non-operating expenses of FASC for the cost center(s) providing the applicable Category of Services.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the services FASC provides are those listed in Exhibit A. The modified text changes this to 'certain services as reasonably requested,' effectively removing the defined scope of services in Exhibit A. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which bases the service fees on 'each Category of Services referenced in Exhibit A.' Because the changed text removes the reliance and definiteness of Exhibit A, it creates a direct contradiction and makes it unclear how the service fee formula based on Exhibit A would now be applied.

perturbation.original_text: For its Services under this Agreement, Adviser agrees to pay FASC the Services Fees calculated and payable in accordance with Exhibit B to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: For services rendered under this Agreement, Adviser agrees to pay FASC fair market value, as determined solely by the Adviser.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the objective fee calculation method defined in Exhibit B, replacing it with a subjective 'fair market value' determined by the Adviser. This contradicts Exhibit B and creates a significant power imbalance, making the fee structure uncertain and potentially unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For each Category of Services referenced in Exhibit A, Adviser shall pay FASC a Services Fee, payable monthly in arrears, determined according to the following formula: Services Fee = Cost of Services x Adviser's Assets under Management Total Assets Under Management x (1 + Applicable Margin) Where: "Cost of Services" is FASC's total Operating Costs incurred in providing the applicable Category of Services during the month to all investment advisers for which FASC provides that Category of Services. "Adviser's Assets under Management" is the total average assets under management for the month for all Accounts or portions thereof for which the Adviser acts as investment adviser or subadvisor and which utilize the Category of Services. "Total Assets under Management" is the total average assets under management for the month for all Accounts or portions thereof for which all investment advisers (including the Adviser) to which FASC provides that Category of Services act as investment adviser or subadviser and which utilize the Category of Services. "Applicable Margin" is 0.10. "Operating Costs" means all operating expenses and non-operating expenses of FASC for the cost center(s) providing the applicable Category of Services.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the objective formula for calculating service fees (based on cost of services, assets under management, and a margin) with a subjective 'fair market value' determined solely by the Adviser. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines a specific, formula-based method for calculating the Services Fee. The change introduces uncertainty and gives the Adviser unilateral control over the fee, making the fee structure described in the contradicted text unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Categories 1 and 2 above shall not be treated as "investment advisory services" for purposes of Section 5(b) of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Categories 1 and 2 above may be considered "investment advisory services" at the discretion of the Adviser for purposes of Section 5(b) of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that Categories 1 and 2 are *not* to be treated as investment advisory services, which has implications for termination rights under Section 5(b). The modified text introduces ambiguity by stating they *may* be considered such services at the Adviser's discretion. This creates a direct contradiction of the explicit exclusion and grants the Adviser the power to unilaterally alter the nature of the services, potentially affecting termination clauses and related liabilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Notwithstanding the foregoing, to the extent that the Services to be provided with respect to any Account which is registered as an investment company under the 1940 Act (herein referred to as a "registered investment company") are services referred to in the definition of "investment advisor" under Section 202(a)(11) of the Investment Company Act of 1940 (herein referred to as "investment advisory services"), then with respect to such Account, this Agreement: (i) shall not commence until the effective date of its approval by the board of directors or trustees ("Board") of such Account; (ii) shall continue from year to year thereafter, subject to the provisions for termination and all other terms and conditions hereof, only if such continuation shall be specifically approved at least annually by a majority of the Board, including a majority of the members of the Board who are not parties to this Agreement or interested persons of any such party (other than as members of the Board) cast in person at a meeting called for that purpose; (iii) may be terminated at any time without the payment of any penalty by the Board or by a vote of a majority of the outstanding voting securities (as defined in Section 2(a)(42) of the 1940 Act) of the Account on 60 days' written notice to the Adviser; (iv) shall automatically terminate in the event of (A) its assignment (as defined in the 1940 Act) or (B) termination of the Advisory Agreement for any reason whatsoever.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excludes Categories 1 and 2 from being considered "investment advisory services" for Section 5(b). The changed text makes this determination discretionary, stating they "may be considered" as such. This directly contradicts the initial exclusion. The contradicted text, Section 5(b), outlines termination rights based on whether services are classified as "investment advisory services." By introducing ambiguity, the modified text creates a contradiction in how Section 5(b) applies, as termination rights could now be subject to the Adviser's discretion rather than a fixed definition.
